UÔłłGL2(╬Ť).This gives the first filtered Nielsen layer. The history records this as the first obstruction vanishing and as the fixed first-layer matrix used in the later finite Nielsen data.

3. Non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled

After the H1 change by U, the higher correction equations split idempotent-by-idempotent.On (1ÔłĺE)╬Ť and E(1Ôłĺf)╬Ť, one coefficient is a unit. Hence every higher error on those pieces is killed by ordinary filtered IA corrections.Thus all genuinely delicate obstruction theory is concentrated in the Ef-local piece.

4. The residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower closes On the residual Ef-piece, the problem becomes the completed mod-2, s=1 Magnus equationS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2,whereS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)Ôłĺ1Y1Ôłĺ2Y03Y0q,and X=1+A,┬áY=1+B.LetLÔłÖÔŐéF2ÔčĘÔčĘA,BÔčęÔčębe the free restricted Lie algebra. If the first nonzero residual error occurs in homogeneous degree n, then it is primitive, hence lies in Ln. The ordinary correction boundary isÔłénÔłĺ1:LnÔłĺ1ÔŐĽLnÔłĺ1ÔćĺLn,(U,V)ÔćŽ[A,U]+[B,V].

The residual recursion isLn/imÔłénÔłĺ1Ôëů{0,Ln/2[2],n┬áodd,n┬áeven.

The sweep proves the uniform root-availability statementCm=Lm (mÔëą5),so every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression WÔćŽW[2], and every odd layer is an ordinary boundary. Thus the residual obstruction tower vanishes to all orders. The attached history records this as the residual induction closing, not merely as a finite-layer computation.

5. The Ef-coefficient tower closesLet

R=Ef╬ŤÔëůZ2 [[t]],t=sÔłĺ1.

Before normalization, the Ef-local row is(s2+1,sÔłĺ1)=(2+2t+t2,t).

But it is exactly Nielsen-equivalent over R to (2,t),because(s2+1)Ôłĺ(s+1)(sÔłĺ1)=2.

Explicitly,(s2+1,sÔłĺ1)(1Ôłĺ(s+1)01)=(2,t).

So the apparent 2t+t2 term is a coordinate artifact.Nowgr(2,t)

RÔëůF2 [╬▒,╬▓],╬▒=in(2),╬▓=in(t),and the normalized coefficient row is the regular Koszul row (╬▒,╬▓).

The first t-layer R/(2,t2) is contractible: there the row is (0,t), and a second-coordinate correction kills any leading t-tail without creating a same-layer restricted-square obstruction.

The first 2-Bockstein layer is controlled byHd(2):=Ld[2]/im╬Şd0,where╬Şd0:ker(LdÔŐĽLd[A,Ôłĺ]+[B,Ôłĺ]Ld+1)ÔćĺLd[2],(u,v)ÔćŽu[2]+v[2].

The sweep establishesBdÔćáHd(2)(dÔëą4).The base range 4ÔëĄdÔëĄ8 is supported by the nonlinear certificates D.1ÔÇôD.4, and dÔëą9 follows from the coefficient-safe induction. The history records the coefficient tower as closed by these first-layer maps plus the normalized KoszulÔÇôWitt cascade: actual pro-2 representatives produce Witt cascades, and with Magnus root degree ordered first, every forced tail is triangularly pushed to a later layer, where either the first 2-Bockstein maps or the residual induction kill it.

6. Certificate ledger The nonlinear certificate blocks supporting the first coefficient layer are now closed in the sweep ledger:

Status explicit┬áBockstein┬áwitnesses explicit┬álower-base┬áwitness stack-rank┬á60Ôćĺ61explicit┬áquotient┬áwitness The last previously open executable item was D.3. The attached history records that the verifier computed rankD5,6,7=60,and rank(D5,6,7╬╝4B4)=61.

Since H4(2)is one-dimensional, this proves that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed ╬╝4=1 scalar to ╬╝4=0.This is important structurally: the d=4 coefficient issue is not solved by hammering one fixed tail. It is solved because the full lower-plus-continuation torsor moves the apparent obstruction, just as the residual degree-16 obstruction was moved by lower-base freedom. The history explicitly identifies this as the final executable certificate closing the finite coefficient nonemptiness argument.

7. Finite filtered Nielsen compactness

For a degree cutoff N and open two-sided ideal JÔŐé╬Ť, set

M=╬ŤÔčĘÔčĘX0,X1ÔčęÔčę,I=(X0,X1),andPN,J:=PÔłę(1+IN+JM).

Then P/PN,J is a finite 2-group andN,JÔőéPN,J=1.

Define SN,J to be the finite set of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data(y0,y1,c¤â,c¤ä)Ôłł(P/PN,J)4 such that:the first Magnus layer of y0,y1 is UmodJ;c¤â,c¤ä satisfy the finite cocycle relation encoding ¤ä¤â=¤ä2;the induced twisted substitution sends ¤üS=rSÔłĺ1 to rH modulo PN,J;the substitution is filtered.Each such datum gives an actual finite over-G automorphism: since the first layer is invertible, the finite Frattini criterion applies to the finite 2-group P/PN,J. This avoids any appeal to Hopficity of the full infinite-rank pro-2 group P. The history records this finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle compactness repair explicitly.The residual induction, coefficient KoszulÔÇôWitt induction, and certificates D.1ÔÇôD.4 implySN,JţÇá=Ôłů for every finite coefficient-and-degree truncation.

The sets SN,J are functorial under refinement. Since they are finite and nonempty, compactness givesN,JlimSN,JţÇá=Ôłů.A compatible inverse-limit point yields a continuous filtered Nielsen automorphism ╬▒:PÔćĺP and a continuous cocycle c:GÔćĺP.

Together they define an over-G automorphism ╬Ž:F=PÔőŐGÔćĺFby ╬Ž(pg)=╬▒(p)c(g)g.

At every finite level,╬Ž(¤üS)ÔëírH (modPN,J).

Intersecting over all N,J gives╬Ž(rSÔłĺ1)=rH inside F.

Therefore ╬Ž(ÔčĘÔčĘrSÔčęÔčęF)=ÔčĘÔčĘrHÔčęÔčęF.

Passing to quotients gives╬ôS=F/ÔčĘÔčĘrSÔčęÔčęFÔëůF/ÔčĘÔčĘrHÔčęÔčęF=╬ôH.

Because ╬Ž is over G, the descended isomorphism satisfies¤ÇHÔłś╬Ž=¤ÇS.Thus╬ôSÔëůG╬ôH.

Audit status Within the attached sweep ledger, no structural mathematical gap remains. The remaining caveat is external reproducibility: the history records verifier outputs and rank certificates, but the actual verifier scripts or full matrices are not attached here. For a standalone paper, those should be included in an appendix. The latest history says exactly this: the sweep proves the over-G isomorphism, while the remaining issue is presentation-level reproducibility outside the transcript.

ESTABLISHED: There is a continuous over-G automorphism ╬Ž:FÔćĺF with ╬Ž(rSÔłĺ1)=rH. Hence ╬Ž identifies the closed normal relator subgroups and descends to an isomorphism ╬ôSÔëůG╬ôH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The remaining gap is external reproducibility/presentation: a standalone appendix should include the verifier scripts or explicit matrices for the recorded rank certificates, especially the D.3 stack-rank 60Ôćĺ61.

NEXT: Produce the final paper-style write-up with theorem-proof formatting and an appendix containing: the H1 Nielsen matrix, residual restricted-Lie induction, coefficient KoszulÔÇôWitt induction, certificates D.1ÔÇôD.4, the D.3 stack-rank output, and the finite filtered Nielsen compactness descent.[TURN57-POLISHED-STANDALONE-PROOF-ISO]
